Once you are familiarised with the environment, we will get into more details in later 
lessons. So what is Tableau? Tableau is a visual analytics platform that empowers 
organisations to understand and utilise [data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-Data-Science-AI/blob/main/Trimester%201/Data%20Visualization%20%26%20Storytelling/W10 - Module 4c. Python Bokeh package - Module 4c. Python Bokeh package/L29/Decorating%20the%20Visuals.md#data))))) types within 
the same environment. Similarly, Tableau's power comes from its intuitive platform 
which is very easy to use and creates most flexibility for the analyst to design 
visualisations. 
  
According to Gartner, Tableau is the gold standard for intuitive and interactive visual 
analysis. And finally, Tableau dashboards are very easy for launching in production. 
So here I would like to take a small pause and explain that while we are developing 
the insights and knowledge about Tableau, we will not generally be discussing about 
how to set up this in the production environment. 
  
This you can learn in the later parts of the course where in your particular work

Python Bokeh package - Module 4c. Python Bokeh package/L29/Decorating%20the%20Visuals.md#data))))) and 
decisions are made on this. But we will be now only looking at the basic features of 
Tableau so that you are familiarised with the environment and later on connect it into 
the production environment. However, when you want to connect to a production 
environment, Tableau dashboards are very easy to be launched in production. 
  
And Salesforce company owns Tableau and it is provided with a various range of 
options that we will see in a while. You have the free version which is Tableau public 
and you have the paid version and [advanced version](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-Data-Science-AI/blob/main/Trimester%201/Data%20Visualization%20%26%20Storytelling/W10 - Module 4c. where there are high-end features which are available on a subscription basis for the 
developers. So it is an entire range of product features. 
  
But for the purposes of this course, we will be using the free version where you can 
learn the basics and learn the techniques of how to build visualisations in Tableau and 
take it from there. One of the main features is like Tableau allows customers or the 
users to spend more time on [data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-[Data](https://github.com/Balasubramanian-pg/MSC.-Data-Science-AI/blob/main/Trimester%201/Data%20Visualization%20%26%20Storytelling/W10 - Module 4c.
